task_completion.description: The CT Cranium DICOM scan is loaded into the medical imaging software (InVesalius).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius interface is open, displaying the axial, coronal, and sagittal views of a human skull.

task_completion.description: A linear measurement is drawn for the maximum transverse (left-to-right) diameter of the skull.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible measurement line spanning horizontally (left-to-right) across the inner cortical surfaces of the skull in the viewer (typically the axial or coronal view). The measurement label should display a value greater than 80 mm.

task_completion.description: A linear measurement is drawn for the maximum anteroposterior (front-to-back) diameter of the skull.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible measurement line spanning vertically or diagonally (front-to-back) across the inner cortical surfaces of the skull in the viewer (typically the axial or sagittal view). The measurement label should display a value greater than 80 mm.

task_completion.description: The project is saved to the specified path: /home/ga/Documents/cranial_measurements.inv3
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Project As' dialog box showing the file name 'cranial_measurements.inv3' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory, or the main application window title bar updated to show 'cranial_measurements.inv3'.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of the medical imaging software to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with InVesalius menus, measurement tools, and save dialogs.

integrity.description: The measurements were actually drawn on the DICOM scan using the software's built-in tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Measurement lines and text labels appear as native overlays within the InVesalius 2D/3D viewer panes, not as text typed into an external editor or generic image manipulation tool.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the GUI measurement process.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not used to directly generate or mock the .inv3 project file or inject measurement data without using the InVesalius interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/cranial_measurements.inv3'. The task requires exactly 2 linear measurements (transverse and anteroposterior) to be drawn on the CT Cranium scan using InVesalius. Both measurements must have a value greater than 80.0 mm to be considered valid cranial dimensions. Look for the measurement lines to span from the inner cortical surface to the opposite inner cortical surface.
